label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Jennifer could have gone to the swimming pool. The question gives a list of times when different people saw her doing various activities, and we need to find the time window when she wasn't doing any of those things, but also before the pool closed at 10pm. First, let me list out all the known activities with their times: 1. Jennifer woke up at 5am. (This is just a starting point, not an activity that occupies her time.) 2. Richard saw her working at the office from 5am to 8am. 3. Samantha saw her reading at the library from 12pm to 1pm. 4. Betty saw her buying a bike at the bike shop from 1pm to 2pm. 5. Sarah saw her buying a phone at the electronics store from 2pm to 9pm. 6. Thomas saw her walking towards the Statue of Liberty from 9pm to 10pm. The swimming pool was closed after 10pm, so Jennifer had to go before that. Now, let's map out her schedule chronologically: - 5am to 8am: Working at the office - 8am to 12pm: Not accounted for yet - 12pm to 1pm: Reading at the library - 1pm to 2pm: Buying a bike - 2pm to 9pm: Buying a phone - 9pm to 10pm: Walking to the Statue of Liberty So, the only time window when Jennifer isn't doing any of the listed activities is between 8am and 12pm. She woke up at 5am and was working until 8am, and then the next activity is at 12pm at the library. So between 8am and 12pm, she's not seen doing anything. This would be the only time window when she could have gone to the swimming pool, as long as she did it before 10pm (which this time window is). Looking at the options: (A) 8am to 12pm - This
